source_title: Wild mit Spaetzle, Rotkraut und glasierten Maronen
normalized_title: Venison with Spaetzle, Red Cabbage, and Glazed Chestnuts
query: Central European venison with spaetzle, braised red cabbage, and glazed chestnuts

draft.title: Venison with Spaetzle, Red Cabbage, and Glazed Chestnuts
draft.description: Classic Central European venison served with homemade spaetzle, braised red cabbage, and sweet glazed chestnuts for a rich and hearty meal.

ingredients.name: venison steak
ingredients.note: preferably from the leg or shoulder

steps.text: Season venison steaks with salt and pepper. Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat and sear steaks on both sides until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Add red cabbage, grated apple, bay leaf, cloves, juniper berries, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, and water. Stir well.
Return venison to the pan, cover, and braise on low heat for about 1 hour until meat is tender and cabbage is soft.
Meanwhile, prepare spaetzle dough by mixing flour, eggs, lukewarm milk, and a pinch of salt until smooth. Let rest for 10 minutes.

Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Press spaetzle dough through a spaetzle maker or colander into the boiling water. Cook until spaetzle float to the surface, then remove with a slotted spoon.
Melt butter in a pan and toss cooked spaetzle in it to lightly brown and add flavor.
For glazed chestnuts, melt butter in a small pan, add chestnuts and brown sugar, and cook gently until chestnuts are glazed and shiny.
Remove bay leaf, cloves, and juniper berries from the cabbage. Serve venison with spaetzle, red cabbage, and glazed chestnuts on the side.
